baseline_escalation.description: Minimal task scoring for state-geometry measurement only; diagnose, do not benchmark.
Compact repair scoring with repair-family-specific controls.
Longer lengths, more baselines. Only if compact repair passes.

baselines: all prior confirmed baselines plus mechanism ablations and leakage guards
baseline_escalation.description: Compact regression, mechanism ablations, leakage guards, freeze decision.

claim_boundary: This contract locks the Tier 7.7 low-rank state repair campaign without implementing or scoring any repair candidate. It predeclares failure modes, repair families, controls, metrics, outcome classes, and baseline-escalation rules. Tier 7.8 and Tier 7.9 are explicitly queued behind the 7.7 repair campaign and must not activate until 7.7 routes there. No mechanism promotion, baseline freeze, public usefulness claim, or hardware/native transfer is authorized from this contract.

contract.decision_boundary: This contract locks the Tier 7.7 low-rank state repair campaign without implementing or scoring any repair candidate. It predeclares failure modes, repair families, controls, metrics, outcome classes, and baseline-escalation rules. Tier 7.8 and Tier 7.9 are explicitly queued behind the 7.7 repair campaign and must not activate until 7.7 routes there. No mechanism promotion, baseline freeze, public usefulness claim, or hardware/native transfer is authorized from this contract.
contract.hypothesis: The low-rank state collapse (PR near 2) is caused by one or more specific architectural pressures. A controlled localization gate can identify the primary cause, enabling a targeted repair instead of blind tuning.
contract.null_hypothesis: The low-rank collapse is an irreducible property of the current architecture at its current capacity and cannot be materially improved without adding more neurons, external random projection, or nonlinear-lag features that generic controls can match.

prior_evidence.77j: low_rank_collapse_confirmed; PR stayed around 2 and readout collapse did not explain it
prior_evidence.77l: partitioned-driver repair improved task score but did not sufficiently increase dimensionality
prior_evidence.77n: generic random projection / nonlinear-lag controls explained the partitioned-driver gain
prior_evidence.77q: CRA-native temporal interface helped current CRA but still lost to strong generic controls
prior_evidence.77s: temporal-basis interface promoted only as bounded engineering utility, not a CRA-specific mechanism

contract.question: Which measured failure mode should the next repair target, and what would count as a real state-dimensionality repair rather than another generic feature win?

do_not_rules: start with a repair implementation before 7.7t contract passes
run 7.8 morphology as active before 7.7 routes there
run lifecycle/evolution before state repair or morphology route decision
use public adapters to rescue a failed standardized core gate
claim mechanism promotion from score gain alone
claim state repair from PR gain alone
freeze a baseline from a contract or compact score
port to hardware/native C before software usefulness survives controls

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Compare state PR when each polyp partition receives independent input channels vs a single shared driver.
failure_modes.description: Common input drive forces units into the same principal modes.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Track per-neuron weight/filter similarity over training; compare fixed-random-weight vs learned-weight PR and pairwise correlations.
failure_modes.description: Learning dynamics make many units converge to similar filters over time.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Measure PR with and without inhibition; compare reduced-inhibition and no-inhibition diagnostic variants.
failure_modes.description: WTA or inhibition collapses state into a small number of dominant modes.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Measure state PR vs input channel count; compare per-input-channel contribution to state variance.
failure_modes.description: State never receives enough independent causal channels.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Compare block-sparse, small-world, and all-to-all recurrent graph PR; measure recurrent weight eigen spectrum.
failure_modes.description: Eigen spectrum / sparse graph structure supports only a few useful modes.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Compare state PR with trophic pressure enabled vs disabled; measure per-polyp activity variance distribution vs trophic counter.
failure_modes.description: Survival/selection pressure suppresses diverse but initially weak states.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Measure state PR vs readout-budgeted PR; compare linear readout vs richer readout observability.
failure_modes.description: Recurrent state may be richer internally than the readout exposes.

failure_modes.candidate_probe: Count per-neuron saturation/clipping events; measure state norm distribution; compare with/without activation clipping.
failure_modes.description: Activity is dynamic but constrained to a narrow manifold by numerical bounds.

metrics.description: Effective state dimensionality; target PR >= 4.0 or >= 2x current baseline.
metrics.threshold_at_128_state: PR >= 4.0 for strong repair candidate; PR >= 2.5 for diagnostic progress

metrics.description: Number of PCA components needed for 95% variance.
Number of PCA components needed for 99% variance.
Fraction of variance in the first principal component.
Eigenvalue distribution of state covariance matrix.
Geomean MSE on the locked Lorenz task.
metrics.threshold_at_128_state: >= 10% improvement vs current CRA and temporal-utility reference

metrics.description: Geomean MSE on the locked Mackey-Glass task.
metrics.threshold_at_128_state: no > 10% material regression vs current baseline

metrics.description: Geomean MSE on the repaired NARMA10 task.
metrics.threshold_at_128_state: no > 10% material regression vs current baseline

metrics.description: Geomean MSE across Mackey-Glass, Lorenz, and repaired NARMA10.
metrics.threshold_at_128_state: improve or stable vs current baseline

metrics.description: Pairwise correlation matrix of state dimensions.
Cosine similarity of learned weight vectors over time.
Readout weight concentration / effective readout degrees of freedom.
Fraction of neurons at saturation bounds.

nonclaims: not language, AGI, or ASI evidence

outcome_classes.action: Route to Repair Family B (independent causal subspace drivers) in 7.7v.
Route to Repair Family D (plasticity anti-homogenization) in 7.7v.
Route to Repair Family A (diversity-preserving state dynamics) in 7.7v.
Route to Repair Family C (recurrent topology/spectrum repair) in 7.7v.
Route to Repair Family A or D depending on whether the compression is activity-based or plasticity-based.
Implement a richer readout diagnostic; if confirmable as the primary cause, route to 7.7v as a separate readout-observability repair.
Fix activation bounds/normalization; rerun 7.7u diagnostics before committing a repair family.
Prioritize the failure mode with the largest PR-sensitivity to intervention; if still inconclusive, try Family A (broadest applicability).
Preserve as bounded utility evidence; do not promote as a state repair.
outcome_classes.description: Task score improves but PR/rank remain collapsed.

outcome_classes.action: Preserve as geometric evidence; do not promote until usefulness gate passes.
outcome_classes.description: PR/rank improve but benchmark usefulness does not.

outcome_classes.action: Repair candidate is not CRA-specific; return to 7.7 to localize a different failure mode.
outcome_classes.description: Random projection or nonlinear-lag still explains the improvement.

outcome_classes.action: Preserve as utility evidence; no mechanism promotion or baseline freeze.
outcome_classes.description: Useful and safe, but not CRA-specific mechanism evidence.
Geometry, usefulness, and attribution all pass.

prerequisite.description: Tier 7.7s promoted temporal-basis interface as bounded engineering utility; Tier 7.7t follows as the next contract.

repair_families.goal: Prevent units from collapsing into the same dominant modes.
mechanism_candidates: anti-synchrony penalty or local inhibitory balancing
repair_families.name: diversity_preserving_state_dynamics

repair_families.goal: Give recurrent units independent causal views without relying on external random projection.
mechanism_candidates: delay-line diversity with same feature budget
input dropout / channel masking during training to force specialization
repair_families.name: independent_causal_subspace_drivers

repair_families.goal: Make the recurrent graph support more useful independent temporal modes.
mechanism_candidates: block-sparse recurrent modules with weak cross-links
repair_families.name: recurrent_topology_spectrum_repair

repair_families.goal: Keep learning from making every unit converge to the same filter.
repair_families.name: plasticity_anti_homogenization

repair_families.goal: Create state diversity through heterogeneous polyp templates.
repair_families.name: morphology_template_variability_route
repair_families.route_after_contract: Tier 7.8 contract only if 7.7u localizes bottleneck to template diversity or families A-D fail cleanly

repair_queue.activation_condition: Failure mode localized to this family's target mechanism.
repair_queue.goal: Prevent units from collapsing into the same dominant modes.

repair_queue.goal: Keep learning from making every unit converge to the same filter.

repair_queue.activation_condition: Failure mode localized to this family's target mechanism.

repair_queue.name: morphology_template_variability_route
repair_queue.next_tier: Tier 7.8 contract only if 7.7u localizes bottleneck to template diversity or families A-D fail cleanly

route_conditions.condition: 7.7u localizes bottleneck to lack of intrinsic unit/template diversity.
route_conditions.requirement: Tier 7.7u must explicitly confirm this failure mode before 7.8 activates.

route_conditions.condition: Repair families A-D fail cleanly and morphology is the next best hypothesis.
route_conditions.requirement: A 7.7 closeout contract must explicitly route to morphology.

route_conditions.condition: A 7.7 closeout contract explicitly routes the low-rank repair campaign to morphology.
7.7 low-rank repair is fixed or formally bounded, AND 7.8 static morphology is tested or bypassed.
route_conditions.requirement: Lifecycle must not activate until state repair and morphology decisions are resolved.

stopping_rules.action: Execute 7.7 closeout contract; narrow claim to bounded temporal utility plus strong Mackey signal with unresolved Lorenz bottleneck.
stopping_rules.rule: Three distinct repair families fail to increase state dimensionality and usefulness under locked controls.

stopping_rules.action: Execute 7.7 closeout contract; the architecture has an unresolved state bottleneck that generic controls can match.
stopping_rules.rule: Every useful score gain remains explained by random projection or nonlinear-lag controls.

stopping_rules.action: Execute 7.7 closeout contract; the benefit is a capacity confound, not a mechanism repair.
stopping_rules.rule: State geometry improves only by adding capacity that matched controls can reproduce.

tier: Tier 7.7t - Low-Rank State Repair Campaign Contract
